Home»Sport»Paris Olympics 2024»Gerda Steyn ramps up preparation for Paris Olympics 2024 marathon

South Africa’s road running darling Gerda Steyn is hard at work preparing for the marathon at the 2024 Paris Olympics.

South Africa’s road running darlingGerda Steynis hard at work preparing for themarathonat the2024 Paris Olympics.

Fresh off her record-breakingComrades Marathonvictory, Steyn will now turn her attention to the42.195kmdistance in Paris in August.

The women’s marathon is set forSunday, 11 August– in exactly54days’ time.

Mark that date in your diaryNOW!

As reported byThe South African website, Team SA will havethreerepresentatives in the women’s marathon in Paris.

Steyn finished 15th in the marathon at the2020 Tokyo Olympics, 4:50 behind Kenyan winnerPeres Jepchirchir.

The 34-year-old holds the South African marathon record of2:24:03.

Steyn will be joined on the start line byCian OldknowandIrvette van Zyl, who will be attending her third Olympics.

Steyn took toInstagramon Tuesday to write:The next block of work is coming in hot! ☄️

There was not much time wasted on the post race blues, because for the next 8 weeks, every single day counts!🤩

Looking forward to getting back to the mountains soon for the final push until#Paris2024… 🇫🇷✨🔜

BRB got to go and charge my phone for overdoing the Alpine picture taking 🔋and Strava uploading.. 🤣

Have a wonderful week ahead!!



A post shared by Gerda Steyn (@gerda_steyn)





Meanwhile, SteynwonherthirdComrades Marathon title – and second ‘up’ run – in a new record time of 5:49:46 on Sunday.

She picked upR2 075 000for her win, courtesy ofR825 000for winning,R500 000for the new record,R500 000for a fastest ever average pace,R200 000for being a South African winner andR50 000for the Cell C Hot Spot incentive.

The race started at05:30at the Durban City Hall and the final cut-off gun sounded 12 hours later at17:30at the Hollywoodbets Scottsville Racecourse.

The official route distance was85.91km, making this year’s race theshortestever Comrades Marathon.

Earlier, as reported byThe South African website, the Netherlands’Piet Wiersmawonthe men’s title.

Wiersma, 26, crossed the finish line in an unofficial time of 5:25:01.

South Africa’sDan Moselakwefinished second in 5:25:45, with Ethiopia’sDegefa Lafeborounding out the podium in 5:27:49.

Wiersma finished second in last year’s ‘down’ run by just three seconds.

His 2023 time was the fastest ever by a Comrades Marathondebutant.

He wonR825 000for his efforts and was the first non-South African winner of the race since Zimbabwe’sStephen Muzhingitriumphed in 2011.

Win – R825 000

Record – R500 000

Best average pace – R500 000

SA athlete – R200 000

KwaZulu-Natal athlete – R60 000

Cell C Hot Spot – R50 000

TOTAL: R2 135 000

Men

Leonid Shvetsov(Russia) – 5.24.39 (set in 2008)

Women

Gerda Steyn (South Africa) – 5.49:46 (set in 2024)
